body_txt: Something really bad (such as horribly out-of-tune singing or violin playing, bad poetry , a perceived ugly character, halitosis, a terrible movie, or Puns ) has the capacity to traumatize those exposed to it or even cause physical damage. The horribleness of it can cause people to have nightmares, need therapy, go blind or deaf (even if a sound is merely annoying or dissonant, not loud), or worse. It may even destroy buildings or blow up cities. Can take the form of a Brown Note , but there can be other effects than that trope. Sometimes this can be invoked with using the horrible thing as Cool and Unusual Punishment . In Real Life , it's fairly common to at least describe a very bad movie as being "painful to watch", or to say that it "hurts to read" a bad book. The concept of an "eyesore" isn't limited to English either; Japanese has migurushii (見苦しい), literally "painful to look at". Can overlap with Anything but That! , Faux Horrific , Comical Overreacting , Dreadful Musician , Even Evil Has Standards (if a villain decides a work would be too horrible to inflict). A Critic Breakdown will often to invoke this regarding the offending piece. Many a Lame Pun Reaction involves retching in pain. Compare/Contrast Awesomeness Is Volatile .
